market_notes: The Western Australia commercial construction market is experiencing steady growth driven by data centre developments amid rising AI and digital infrastructure demands. Key sectors like data centres and mixed-use projects are reshaping the skyline, with overall Australian construction projected to reach AUD 256 billion by 2030. Recruiters should note the increasing focus on high-tech facilities, potentially leading to skilled labor shortages in specialized roles.

top_3_today.headline: Multiplex advances NEXTDC P1 Perth data centre project
top_3_today.why_it_matters: This $110m data centre project is in advanced stages, indicating ramp-up in workforce needs for completion and operations. It represents a money opportunity for recruiters as Multiplex is actively hiring for roles like Commercial Lead and Construction Lead. Targeting this could secure placements in a high-value sector this week.
top_3_today.best_first_action: LinkedIn message to Multiplex's WA Construction Manager, referencing their recent topping out of NEXTDC P1 Perth and offering candidates for data centre roles.

top_3_today.headline: CDC plans $415m AI-ready data centre in Perth
top_3_today.why_it_matters: Approval of this major data centre signals upcoming construction activity, creating demand for skilled trades and management. Recruiters can capitalize on tendering phases by connecting with potential contractors. This is timely as the project is set for 2026 operations, driving immediate hiring needs.
top_3_today.best_first_action: Email outreach to CDC Data Centres' project leads, highlighting recruitment support for their Perth campus expansion.

top_3_today.headline: Built launches 2026 Graduate Program in Perth
top_3_today.why_it_matters: Built's graduate intake points to company growth and entry-level hiring in commercial construction. This creates opportunities for recruiters to supply talent pipelines. With applications open, quick action could lead to partnerships for filling these roles this week.
top_3_today.best_first_action: Contact Built's HR team via LinkedIn, mentioning their 2026 WA Graduate Program and proposing candidate shortlists.

hot_projects.name: NEXTDC P1 Perth Data Centre
hot_projects.description: A $110m data centre expansion project including Stage Two topping out, focused on high-density computing facilities.

hot_projects.name: CDC Perth Data Centre Campus
hot_projects.description: A 200MW AI and advanced technology data centre campus planned outside Perth, with initial $415m investment.

hot_projects.name: Station Street Commercial Development
hot_projects.description: Multi-level mixed-use development on the former Station Street Markets site in Subiaco.

hot_projects.name: Busselton Shopping Centre Redevelopment
hot_projects.description: Redevelopment of the Busselton Shopping Centre, focusing on commercial retail spaces.

hot_projects.name: Ellenbrook Shopping Centre
hot_projects.description: Construction of a new shopping centre in Ellenbrook, enhancing retail infrastructure.

hiring_signals.why_hot: Multiplex is actively recruiting for data centre-specific roles amid their ongoing NEXTDC P1 Perth project. Job openings include Commercial Lead and Construction Lead, driven by project expansion. This indicates strong hiring momentum in the data centre sector.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: Congratulations on topping out Stage Two of NEXTDC's P1 Perth – I have top-tier candidates ready for your data centre team.

hiring_signals.why_hot: Built has launched its 2026 Graduate Program in Perth, signaling expansion and need for new talent in commercial projects. This is part of their growth in WA's construction market. Visible job posts for graduates suggest broader hiring plans.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: Saw your 2026 WA Graduate Program – let's discuss how I can help fill those entry-level positions with high-potential talent.

hiring_signals.why_hot: Doric is involved in commercial developments like Station Street, driving need for project management staff. Recent project wins indicate growth and potential hiring for skilled roles. Job market listings show openings in commercial carpentry and building.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: With your Station Street project underway, I can connect you with experienced estimators to keep things on track.

hiring_signals.why_hot: Following rebranding to Atlas Building and recent contract wins like Mandurah, BGC is expanding in commercial sectors. This drives hiring for construction roles. General WA job boards show multiple openings in building construction.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: Impressed by your recent $43m contract win – I have site managers available to support your commercial builds.

hiring_signals.why_hot: Georgiou's involvement in WA projects, though mostly civil, includes some commercial aspects leading to hiring needs. Recent contracts suggest workforce expansion. Job listings in Perth indicate demand for builders.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: Noting your recent highway upgrade win, I can provide project managers experienced in large-scale WA builds.

hiring_signals.why_hot: General market growth in commercial sectors is prompting ADCO to hire for ongoing projects. Visible job posts in Perth for construction roles. Expansion announcements imply need for more staff.
hiring_signals.outreach_hook: As WA's commercial market heats up, let's talk about filling your estimator roles with top talent.
